info.name: MAD46 Team 3 — Shopify Storefront GraphQL
info.description:
Storefront API GraphQL collection for the MAD46 iOS Shopify project.

Endpoint: /api/{version}/graphql.json  (NOT /admin/api/)
Auth header: X-Shopify-Storefront-Access-Token

Flow:
1. Set {{storefront_token}} in Variables
2. Run 'Get Shop Info' to verify auth
3. Run 'Login Customer' → copy accessToken → set {{customer_access_token}}
4. Run 'Create Cart' → copy cart.id → set {{cart_id}}

request.description: Spec §5 — Verify auth and get the store base currency for exchange-rate conversion.
Spec §2 — Main product catalog with cursor-based pagination. Pass endCursor into 'after' for the next page.
Spec §2 — Full product detail: images, options/sizes, price, description, rating metafields.
Spec §2 — Alternative to fetching by GID. Handle is the URL slug (e.g. 'classic-white-sneaker').
Spec §2 — Brand/Vendor navigation. Replace 'Nike' with the actual brand. Storefront query syntax: vendor:BrandName
Spec §3 — Sort by price ascending. Storefront API has native PRICE sortKey (unlike Admin API). Set reverse: true for descending.
Spec §3 — Best Seller sorting. Storefront API has native BEST_SELLING sortKey (unavailable in Admin API).
Bonus — Shopify-generated product recommendations based on purchase history and product relationships. Good for a 'You may also like' section on the product detail screen.
Spec §3 — Global search across product titles, descriptions, tags, and vendor names. Returns totalCount for display.
Spec §3 — Realtime autocomplete suggestions as the user types. Fire on every keystroke with debounce (~300ms).
Spec §3 — Main Categories and Sub-Categories. Agree with your team on a naming convention in Shopify admin.
Spec §3 — Filter products by category with sorting. sortKey options: MANUAL, BEST_SELLING, TITLE, PRICE, CREATED, COLLECTION_DEFAULT.
Spec §3 — Fetch a collection by its URL handle instead of GID.
Spec §1 — Create a customer account. After this, Shopify sends the verification email automatically if the store has it configured.
Spec §1 — Login and get a customerAccessToken. Copy accessToken from the response into {{customer_access_token}} variable. Required for all customer-specific operations below.
Spec §1 (Destructive Action) — Invalidates the session token. Show confirmation dialog before calling. Clear the token from local storage after success.
Spec §5 — Full account screen: welcome info, recent order history, saved addresses. One query covers all three.
Spec §5 — Update profile. Note: if email or password is changed, a new customerAccessToken is returned — update your stored token.
Spec §1 — Triggers a password reset email to the user. No token required.
Spec §5 — Add delivery address. firstName, lastName, and phone are required per spec.
Spec §5 — Edit an existing saved address.
Spec §2 (Destructive Action) — Always show a confirmation alert before calling. Spec: 'Execution should be halted until explicit user confirmation is programmatically verified.'
Spec §5 — Mark an address as the default shipping address.
Spec §4 — Creates a cart and associates it with the logged-in customer. Copy cart.id into {{cart_id}}. checkoutUrl is used to redirect to payment.
Spec §4 — Fetch current cart state. cost.totalAmount reflects dynamic pricing after discounts (Spec: Dynamic Pricing Calculation).
Spec §4 — Add a product variant to the existing cart. Can add multiple lines in one call.
Spec §4 — Update quantity for a specific line item. Use cart line id (from Get Cart response), not the variant id. Validate against quantityAvailable before calling (Stock Validation).
Spec §4 (Destructive Action) — Remove a line item. Show confirmation dialog before calling.
Spec §6 — Apply a discount code to the cart. Check discountCodes[].applicable in the response — false means the code exists but doesn't apply to this cart (wrong items, expired, etc).
Spec §5 — Account screen order history sorted newest first. Includes discount breakdown per order.
